calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George Holland for Roberto Jourdan (Petitioner).

The Court raised the issue of funds placed in a bank out of California and over the insured limit. Mr. Holland stated that the Petitioner lives in Seattle, that Probate Code 9700 says that funds may be placed in a California bank, that it does not say they can't be placed in a bank outside of California, that he has discussed it with his Client and that he does not feel it was intentional by his Client. The Court informed Counsel that it modified the order to reflect that the Executor's accountings are confirmed except for depositing funds out of California and over the insured amount. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Final Distribution of Waiver of Account, etc. (modified order signed in open Court).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Continued To Aug-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Holland for (Petitioner) Roberto Jourdan.

Mr. Holland reported that he substituted in on Friday, that he is in the process of reviewing the materials, expects that he will need to amend the petition and that he has not seen any of the examiner's notes. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to August 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-22-2015 Continued To May-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roberto Jourdan in Propria Persona (via court call). Virginia Jourdan (beneificary) in Propria Persona.

The Court asked why there has been no response to the Examiner's notes. Mr. Jourdan stated that he has been working on this himself, doing the best he can and that he thought everything had been completed. The Court stated that it will continue this matter, that Mr. Jourdan will need to have counsel in place by the next hearing, that there are still issues to resolve and that if there is not counsel at the next hearing the court may act on the information that has been provided and that the relief being sought may not be granted. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to May 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
